Event: Sméagol murders Déagol for the Ring
Explanation: He gains the Ring and extended life at the cost of killing his cousin and starting his own ruin.

Event: Sméagol examines and hooks a live bait-worm with a smile
Explanation: He secures fishing bait for himself; minimal broader impact beyond routine hunting.

Event: Sméagol spends centuries alone in the mountains with only the Ring
Explanation: His isolation and dependence on the Ring devastate his mind and life without affecting others directly.

Event: Sauron’s forces torture Gollum for information about the Ring
Explanation: They likely gain intelligence at the cost of severely harming Gollum.

Event: The Ring extends Gollum’s lifespan to ~600 years
Explanation: He gains vast longevity despite corruption already in place, others largely unaffected yet.

Event: The Ring drives Sméagol to kill his cousin and abandon normal life
Explanation: It pushes him into murder and lifelong ruin with no real gain beyond obsession.

Event: Bilbo takes the Ring and leaves Gollum in the dark
Explanation: Bilbo secures safety and the Ring, while Gollum loses his “Precious” and is left desperate.

Event: Gollum plays a riddle game with Bilbo
Explanation: The contest delays violence and gives both a chance to get what they want with their wits.

Event: Gollum decides to kill Bilbo after losing the riddle game
Explanation: The intent to kill risks exposing himself and fails, yielding no benefit and harm to another.

Event: Gollum attempts to kill Bilbo out of spite when he loses
Explanation: The murder attempt fails and only increases his loss and danger to others.

Event: Gollum looks at his reflection and realizes Bilbo has the Ring
Explanation: He correctly deduces the Ring’s location, improving his informational position.

Event: Gollum stalks and trails the Fellowship to retake the Ring
Explanation: He pursues a chance to reclaim the Ring while posing a covert threat to them.

Event: Gollum survives starvation, marshes, torture, and a cliff fall through sheer determination
Explanation: His tenacity preserves his life and capacity to act, with limited direct effect on others.

Event: Gollum reacts with horror to Elven rope and lembas bread
Explanation: His corruption makes beneficial items agonizing, harming him while blocking help from others.

Event: Gollum, spared by Frodo and Sam, agrees to guide them to Mordor
Explanation: He gains acceptance and purpose while providing crucial guidance toward their goal.

Event: Sméagol takes a shine to Frodo because Frodo is kind to him
Explanation: He emotionally aligns with Frodo, which softens him and supports Frodo’s efforts.

Event: Sméagol banishes the Gollum side for a time
Explanation: Suppressing his darker self improves his behavior and safety for himself and companions.

Event: Gollum serves as token evil teammate and not-always-willing guide
Explanation: He retains a shot at the Ring while materially helping their progress.

Event: Sméagol swears on the Ring to do no harm to Frodo and Sam
Explanation: He gains conditional trust and stability while committing to protect them.

Event: Sméagol uses his oath to argue against Gollum’s wish to turn back to Mordor
Explanation: He safeguards his bond with Frodo and keeps the mission on track.

Event: Frodo and Sam spare Gollum’s life
Explanation: They risk some danger but gain an indispensable guide and instrument of the Ring’s eventual doom.

Event: Sam remains cruel and suspicious toward Gollum even when he behaves well
Explanation: Sam defends Frodo via distrust but worsens relations and helps drive Gollum back to treachery.

Event: Sam finally starts to trust Gollum and calls him “very decent”
Explanation: This brief acceptance eases tension and could have reinforced Gollum’s better side.

Event: Sméagol initially sides with Frodo and seemingly overcomes the Ring’s corruption
Explanation: He moves toward redemption and gives Frodo a loyal ally.

Event: Sméagol shows desire for connection and his former innocent self
Explanation: His yearning for normalcy improves his mental state and potential prosocial behavior.

Event: During the Forbidden Pool scene, Gollum’s darker instincts reassert, and he starts plotting against the Hobbits
Explanation: He sacrifices a chance at real redemption for a risky scheme to regain the Ring.

Event: Faramir and his Rangers capture Gollum roughly
Explanation: They secure a dangerous creature and intel while traumatizing him and damaging the fragile alliance.

Event: Frodo tricks Gollum into being captured by Faramir’s Rangers
Explanation: Frodo protects the broader mission but deeply betrays Gollum’s trust and destabilizes him.

Event: Gollum feels betrayed by Frodo after the Ranger incident
Explanation: His resentment poisons a potentially salvific relationship and drives him toward self-destructive revenge.

Event: Gollum begins plotting to murder Frodo and Sam for the Ring
Explanation: He seeks a huge personal gain at high risk to himself and enormous harm to them.

Event: Sméagol and Gollum argue, with Gollum bullying Sméagol back toward betrayal
Explanation: The darker side sabotages their one path to safety and acceptance for no guaranteed payoff.

Event: Sméagol eventually sides with Gollum outright
Explanation: Choosing his darker self shuts the door on redemption and leads toward his doom.

Event: Gollum manipulates and lies to Frodo and Sam while acting friendly
Explanation: He inches closer to reclaiming the Ring while undermining the trust that sustains him.

Event: Gollum delights in tormenting others and inflicting petty harm
Explanation: He gains emotional gratification at others’ expense while worsening his social standing.

Event: Gollum acts as the main recurring threat to Frodo and Sam’s quest
Explanation: His opposition may net him the Ring if successful, but it constantly endangers them.

Event: Gollum torments Frodo by singing a taunting song in Shelob’s lair
Explanation: The mockery adds psychological harm to Frodo without helping Gollum’s objective.

Event: Gollum gleefully rubs his betrayal in Frodo’s face on Mount Doom’s slope
Explanation: He wastes time and focus on cruelty instead of securing the Ring, aiding his own undoing.

Event: Gollum reacts to Elven bread as if it were filth and refuses it
Explanation: He chooses starvation over nourishing food because of his corruption, while others try to help.

Event: Sméagol blames the Ring for making him want to kill Frodo in Shelob’s lair
Explanation: Shifting blame hinders self-awareness and reform despite Frodo’s pity.

Event: Frodo fights Gollum in Shelob’s lair
Explanation: He defends himself and the Ring but fails to resolve the threat and again spares Gollum.

Event: Sméagol briefly resurfaces in Shelob’s lair, evoking Frodo’s pity
Explanation: His vulnerability elicits mercy and a chance—however squandered—for change.

Event: Frodo spares Gollum in Shelob’s lair
Explanation: He risks immediate danger but preserves the very agent who will later ensure the Ring’s destruction.

Event: Frodo admits the true purpose of the quest (to destroy the Ring) to Gollum
Explanation: Honesty aligns with the mission but destabilizes Gollum, prompting renewed attack.

Event: Upon learning the quest’s purpose, Gollum snaps and attacks Frodo again
Explanation: The attack undermines his chance at acceptance and sets up his own fatal end.

Event: Gollum swears on the Ring then later breaks his oath and tries to kill Frodo
Explanation: Oath-breaking destroys any remaining trust and leads directly to his demise.

Event: Gollum coldly says “Sméagol lied” while choking Frodo
Explanation: The gloating yields no extra gain and cements total moral and relational collapse.

Event: Gollum deceives Frodo and Sam into Shelob’s lair to have them killed
Explanation: He nearly secures the Ring at enormous risk to them and to the world.

Event: Gollum attempts to take the Ring back from Frodo in Mount Doom
Explanation: Though selfishly motivated, his struggle triggers the Ring’s destruction, saving others.

Event: Gollum bites off Frodo’s finger to reclaim the Ring
Explanation: He regains the Ring at last while gravely injuring Frodo and courting danger at the edge.

Event: Gollum dances and exults with the Ring at the Cracks of Doom
Explanation: His careless jubilation near the precipice directly causes his fatal fall.

Event: Gollum falls with the Ring into Mount Doom’s lava, still clinging to it
Explanation: He dies horribly while unintentionally guaranteeing the Ring’s destruction and saving Middle-earth.

Event: As he sinks in lava, Gollum holds the Ring up, trying to keep it from destruction
Explanation: His futile effort intensifies his suffering while inadvertently ensuring the greater good.

Event: Gollum’s attempt to seize the Ring from Frodo causes it to fall into the fire
Explanation: The grab dooms him and destroys his obsession, but it completely saves everyone else.

Event: Gollum upstages Andy Serkis at the MTV Awards and rants about filming
Explanation: He creates a humorous, memorable moment that boosts both his and Serkis’s profile.

Event: Gollum throws shade at Dobby and Peter Jackson in his rant
Explanation: He gains laughs and attention while mildly insulting others’ reputations.

Event: Gollum serves as a Final Boss to heroes trying to throw the Ring in the game
Explanation: He stands between them and success to protect his interest in the Ring.

Event: Gollum acts as Sauron’s de facto heavy by menacing Frodo and Sam
Explanation: He advances the Dark Lord’s implicit interest by threatening the quest while chasing his own.

Event: Gollum’s offscreen stealth movements to appear behind Bilbo
Explanation: His stealth advantages him tactically while unnerving and endangering Bilbo.

Event: Sméagol repeatedly talks to his reflection as a second self
Explanation: His fragmented self-dialogue reflects and reinforces his broken psyche without aiding him.

Event: Gollum stalks Frodo and Sam through three films without a crush
Explanation: This persistent pursuit keeps him close to the Ring while endangering them.

Event: By guiding Frodo and Sam toward Mordor, Gollum almost gives Sauron victory
Explanation: His interference nearly results in the Ring’s recovery and disaster for all with no sure gain.

Event: Gollum’s last intervention at Mount Doom prevents the Ringwraiths reclaiming the Ring
Explanation: His selfish attack unintentionally saves the world while costing him everything.

Event: Bilbo, Frodo, and Gandalf show mercy and pity toward Gollum
Explanation: Their compassion preserves the one agent whose actions finally destroy the Ring.

Event: Gollum lies that “the Ring made us do it” to escape responsibility
Explanation: He seeks to dodge blame and maybe pity while deepening his own self-deception and mistrust.

Event: Gollum obsessively calls the Ring “my Precious” and centers life on it
Explanation: His fixation sustains his identity but entrenches isolation, misery, and self-sabotage.
